item.title: 加入我们#WeCoded 2023！ | Join us for #WeCoded 2023!
item.description:
开发团队的Jess Lee

发表于 3 月 8 日

我很高兴代表开发团队宣布我们的第一个#WeCoded 活动！

在过去的五年里，我们在国际妇女节通过名为#SheCoded 的庆祝活动来庆祝科技界的女性。 随着时间的推移， #SheCoded已成为我们社区在技术成功、不平等待遇、歧视和面对逆境时的勇气等方面经验的强大集合。

今年，我们将庆祝活动的范围扩大到包括所有因性别而在软件开发中代表性不足或被边缘化的人：包括女性、跨性别者、非二元性、不符合性别的人和具有双重精神的人。 我们所有人都在倡导科技行业实现更大的性别平等，因此欢迎并邀请所有人参与#WeCoded，不分性别。

如何参与#WeCoded

分享你的故事

通过选择我们的提示之一为对话做出贡献……或者制作您自己的提示！ 每个参与的人都将收到一张优惠券，用于订购免费的#WeCoded 贴纸包。

写下您的#WeCoded 帖子

几点提醒：

  • 请善待彼此，互相支持。 我们要求您在发帖和发表评论之前查看我们的行为准则。
  • 对自己的话负责，说话前三思，避免假设和“魔鬼拥护者”的争论。
  • 通过提出问题、解释您的观点并记住我们都有不同的生活经历，提供推动对话向前发展的反馈。
  • 如果您看到包含辱骂、仇恨或不宽容语言的帖子或评论，请使用我们的审核工具进行举报。
  • 请阅读我们的社区已经分享的内容，并用您自己的故事或对他人的建议做出同样的回应。

加入对话

我们的团队将在 3 月 8 日至 17 日期间分享有关科技领域性别平等的讨论帖。 留意这些帖子并与您的 #DEVCommunity 成员分享您的想法！

将您的代词添加到您的个人资料

如果您错过了它：您现在可以将您的代词添加到您的 DEV 个人资料中！ 为此，首先确保您已登录，然后导航至您的个人资料设置> 个人 > 代词。

不确定这意味着什么？ 查看此有用的指南以了解更多信息。

购买 WeCoded 商品

为了提高 DEV 社区的知名度并将其与支持女性、跨性别者、非二元性、性别不合格和双重精神技术专业人士的技术亲和团体和组织联系起来，DEV 将把活动期间销售的 WeCoded 商品的所有收益捐赠给以下 501(c)(3) 非营利组织：

关于国际妇女节的说明
与#SheCoded 一样，#WeCoded 恰逢每年 3 月 8 日举行的国际妇女节 (IWD) 。女性平等是科技领域性别平等的关键部分，但这并不是全部。 我们鼓励您在整个#WeCoded 期间以及一年中的每隔一天在 IWD 上庆祝和赋予妇女和女孩科技领域的权力！

感谢大家成为 DEV 社区的一员，并帮助我们使这个空间成为所有程序员的家——我们只能一起做！ 克里斯蒂安费尔南多 - 3 月 8 日

一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 devteam 的操作：

降低 devteam 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning devteam 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Jess Lee for The DEV Team

Posted on Mar 8

   

On behalf of the DEV Team, I’m excited to announce our first #WeCoded event!

For the past five years, we’ve celebrated women in tech on International Women’s Day with a celebration called #SheCoded. Over time, #SheCoded has become a powerful collection of our community’s experiences with technical success, unequal treatment, discrimination, and courage in the face of adversity.

This year, we’re expanding the scope of the celebration to include all people who are underrepresented and otherwise marginalized in software development on the basis of gender: including women, transgender, nonbinary, gender-nonconforming, and two-spirit individuals. It takes all of us to advocate for greater gender equity in the tech industry, so everyone is welcome and invited to participate in #WeCoded regardless of gender.

How to Participate in #WeCoded

Share Your Story

Contribute to the conversation by selecting one of our prompts…or make your own! Everyone who participates will receive a coupon to order a free #WeCoded sticker pack.

Write Your #WeCoded Post

A few reminders:

  • Please be kind and supportive of each other. We ask that you review our Code of Conduct before posting and commenting.
  • Take responsibility for your words, think before you speak, and avoid hypotheticals and “devil’s advocate” arguments.
  • Give feedback that moves the conversation forward by asking questions, explaining your views, and remembering that we all have different life experiences.
  • If you see a post or comment that contains abusive, hateful, or intolerant language, please report it using our moderation tools.
  • Please read what our community has already shared and respond in kind with your own story or advice for others.

Join the Conversation

Our team will be sharing discussion posts on gender equity in tech between March 8–17. Look out for these posts and share your thoughts with your fellow #DEVCommunity members!

Add Your Pronouns to Your Profile

In case you missed it: you can now add your pronouns to your DEV profile! To do this, first make sure you’re logged in, then navigate to your profile settings > Personal > Pronouns.

Not sure what this means? Check out this helpful guide to learn more.

Shop WeCoded Merch

WeCoded is a celebration of individuals who are underrepresented and otherwise marginalized in software development on the basis of gender: including women, transgender, gender non-conforming, and two-spirit people.

In an effort to bring awareness to and connect the DEV community with tech affinity groups and organizations that support women, transgender, nonbinary, gender-nonconforming, and two-spirit tech professionals, DEV will donate all proceeds from WeCoded merchandise sold during the event to the following 501(c)(3) non-profit organizations:

A note on International Women’s Day
Like #SheCoded, #WeCoded coincides with International Women’s Day (IWD), which is held annually on March 8. Women’s equality is a key part of gender equity in tech, but it’s not the whole story. We encourage you to celebrate and empower women and girls in tech on IWD, throughout #WeCoded, and every other day of the year, too!

Thank you all for being part of the DEV Community and helping us make this space a home for all coders — we can only do it together! #WeCoded

Chakrit Nimmanant - Mar 8

Chakrit Nimmanant - Mar 8

Cristian Fernando - Mar 8

Once suspended, devteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

devteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: jess

item.title: 使用 OpenID Connect 和 Apache APISIX 进行身份验证 | Authenticate with OpenID Connect and Apache APISIX
item.description:
许多公司都渴望提供他们的身份提供商：Twitter、Facebook、谷歌等。对于小型企业来说，不必管理身份是一个好处。 但是，我们希望避免被锁定在一个提供商中。 在这篇文章中，我想演示如何使用下面的 Google 使用 OpenID Connect，然后切换到 Azure。

授权开放标准的想法始于 2006 年左右的OAuth 。由于安全问题，OAuth 2.0 取代了初始版本。 第三方应用程序代表自己获取访问权限

身份验证部分非常简单：
它包含有关客户端密码身份验证和一种其他身份验证方法的部分。

授权服务器可以支持匹配其安全要求的任何合适的 HTTP 身份验证方案。 当使用其他身份验证方法时，授权服务器必须定义客户端标识符（注册记录）和

认证方案。

其他身份验证方法

它允许客户端根据授权服务器执行的身份验证来验证最终用户的身份，并以可互操作和类似 REST 的方式获取有关最终​​用户的基本配置文件信息。

该规范套件是可扩展的，允许参与者在对他们有意义时使用可选功能，例如身份数据加密、OpenID 提供者发现和注销。

以下是一些与 OpenID Connect 兼容的身份提供者：

  • 谷歌
  • 微软
  • 苹果
  • 推特
  • 在下文中，我们将从 Google 开始，然后切换到 Azure 来验证我们的设置。

    使用 Apache APISIX 设置 OpenID Connect

    想象一下，我们在 Apache APISIX 后面有一个 Web 应用程序，我们希望使用 OpenID Connect 来保护它。 这是相应的 Docker Compose 文件：

     version: "3" services: apisix: image: apache/apisix:3.1.0-debian #1 ports: - "9080:9080" volumes: - ./apisix/config.yml:/usr/local/apisix/conf/config.yaml:ro #2 - ./apisix/apisix.yml:/usr/local/apisix/conf/apisix.yaml:ro #3 env_file: - .env httpbin: image: kennethreitz/httpbin #4
    1. 配置单一路由
    2. 任何都会做

    一个这样的插件是openid-connect插件，它允许使用 OpenID Connect。

    让我们配置它：

     routes: - uri: /* #1 upstream: nodes: "httpbin:80": 1 #1 plugins: openid-connect: client_id: ${{OIDC_CLIENTID}} #2 client_secret: ${{OIDC_SECRET}} #2 discovery: https://${{OIDC_ISSUER}}/.well-known/openid-configuration #2#3 redirect_uri: http://localhost:9080/callback #4 scope: openid #5 session: secret: ${{SESSION_SECRET}} #6 #END
    1. 通向底层 Web 应用程序的全部路由
    2. 插件配置参数。 值取决于确切的提供者（见下文）
    3. 有关详细信息，请参阅OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 规范
    4. 认证成功后重定向到哪里。 它不得与任何明确定义的路线发生冲突。 该插件在那里创建了一条专用路由以发挥其魔力。
    5. 默认范围
    6. 加密会话数据的密钥。 想放什么就放什么。

    为 OIDC 配置 Google

    与所有云提供商一样，谷歌提供了一个成熟的身份管理解决方案，这可能会让新手望而生畏。 在本节中，我将仅详细介绍为 OIDC 配置它所需的必要步骤。

    在Cloud Console上，创建一个专用项目（或使用现有项目）。

    如果您还没有这样做，请自定义OAuth Consent Screen 。

    在项目上下文中，导航APIs & Services | 凭证。

    然后，按上方菜单栏中的+ CREATE CREDENTIALS按钮。

    在滚动菜单中选择OAuth Client Id 。

    填写字段：

    • 应用类型：网络应用
    • 名称：随便你
    • 授权的重定向 URI： <URL>/callback ，例如， http://localhost:9080/callback

    同样， /callback应该匹配上面的openid-connect插件配置。 请注意，Google 不允许相对 URL，因此如果您需要在不同环境中重用该应用程序，则需要添加每个环境的 URL。 单击创建按钮。

    在上面的 Docker Compose 配置中，使用 Client ID 和 Client Secret 作为OIDC_CLIENTID和OIDC_SECRET 。 我将它们作为环境变量写在.env文件中。

    最后一个缺少的变量是OIDC_ISSUER ：它是accounts.google.com 。 如果您导航到accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-… ，您将看到 OAuth 2.0（以及更多）所需的所有数据。

    此时，我们可以使用docker compose up开始我们的设置。 当我们导航到localhost:9080/时，浏览器将我们重定向到 Google 身份验证页面。 因为我已经通过身份验证，所以我可以选择我的 ID - 我需要一个绑定到我上面创建的项目的组织。

    然后，我就可以自由访问该资源了。

    为 OIDC 配置 Azure

    我的同事 Bobur 已经描述了为 OIDC 配置 Azure 所需执行的所有操作。

    我们只需要更改 OIDC 参数：

    • OIDC_CLIENTID
    • OIDC_SECRET
    • 如果使用新参数重新启动 Docker Compose，根页面现在受 Azure 登录保护。

      结论

      将您的身份验证过程外部化给第三方可能是明智的，但您希望避免将您的基础设施绑定到其专有过程。 没有理由不使用它，因为所有专用身份提供程序（例如 Okta 和 Keycloak）都与 OIDC 兼容。

      这篇文章的完整源代码可以在 GitHub 上找到：

      更进一步：

      最初于 2023 年 3 月 5日发表于A Java Geek

      哥萨克实验室 - 22 年 12 月 14 日

      一旦暂停，apisix 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

      解除暂停后，apisix 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

      一旦取消发布，apisix 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

      如果 apisix 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

      笔记：

      感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 apisix 的方法：

      降低 apisix 的所有帖子的可见度

      报告其他不当行为

      取消标记 apisix 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Lots of companies are eager to provide their identity provider: Twitter, Facebook, Google, etc. For smaller businesses, not having to manage identities is a benefit. However, we want to avoid being locked into one provider. In this post, I want to demo how to use OpenID Connect using Google underneath and then switch to Azure.

OpenID Connect

The idea of an authorization open standard started with OAuth around 2006. Because of a security issue, OAuth 2.0 superseded the initial version. OAuth 2.0 became an IETF RFC in 2012:

The OAuth 2.0 authorization framework enables a third-party application to obtain limited access to an HTTP service, either on behalf of a resource owner by orchestrating an approval interaction between the resource owner and the HTTP service, or by allowing the

third-party application to obtain access on its own behalf

-- RFC 7469 - The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework

OAuth focuses mostly on authorization;
the authentication part is pretty light:
it contains a section about Client Password authentication and one Other Authentication Methods.

The authorization server MAY support any suitable HTTP authentication scheme matching its security requirements. When using other authentication methods, the authorization server MUST define a mapping between the client identifier (registration record) and

authentication scheme.

-- 2.3.2. Other Authentication Methods

OpenID Connect uses OAuth 2.0 and adds the authentication part:

OpenID Connect 1.0 is a simple identity layer on top of the OAuth 2.0 protocol. It allows Clients to verify the identity of the End-User based on the authentication performed by an Authorization Server, as well as to obtain basic profile information about the End-User in an interoperable and REST-like manner.

OpenID Connect allows clients of all types, including Web-based, mobile, and JavaScript clients, to request and receive information about authenticated sessions and end-users. The specification suite is extensible, allowing participants to use optional features such as encryption of identity data, discovery of OpenID Providers, and logout, when it makes sense for them.

-- What is OpenID Connect?

Here are a couple of identity providers that are compatible with OpenID Connect:

  • GitHub
  • Google
  • Microsoft
  • Apple
  • Facebook
  • Twitter
  • Spotify

In the following, we will start with Google and switch to Azure to validate our setup.

Setting up OpenID Connect with Apache APISIX

Imagine we have a web app behind Apache APISIX that we want to secure with OpenID Connect. Here's the corresponding Docker Compose file:

version: "3" services: apisix: image: apache/apisix:3.1.0-debian #1 ports: - "9080:9080" volumes: - ./apisix/config.yml:/usr/local/apisix/conf/config.yaml:ro #2 - ./apisix/apisix.yml:/usr/local/apisix/conf/apisix.yaml:ro #3 env_file: - .env httpbin: image: kennethreitz/httpbin #4
  1. Apache APISIX API Gateway
  2. APISIX configuration - used to configure it statically in the following line
  3. Configure the single route
  4. Webapp to protect. Any will do

Apache APISIX offers a plugin-based architecture. One such plugin is the openid-connect plugin, which allows using OpenID Connect.

Let's configure it:

routes: - uri: /* #1 upstream: nodes: "httpbin:80": 1 #1 plugins: openid-connect: client_id: ${{OIDC_CLIENTID}} #2 client_secret: ${{OIDC_SECRET}} #2 discovery: https://${{OIDC_ISSUER}}/.well-known/openid-configuration #2#3 redirect_uri: http://localhost:9080/callback #4 scope: openid #5 session: secret: ${{SESSION_SECRET}} #6
#END
  1. Catch-all route to the underlying web app
  2. Plugin configuration parameters. Values depend on the exact provider (see below)
  3. OpenID Connect can use a Discovery endpoint to get all necessary OAuth endpoints. See OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 spec for more information
  4. Where to redirect when the authentication is successful. It mustn't clash with any of the explicitly defined routes. The plugin creates a dedicated route there to work its magic.
  5. Default scope
  6. Key to encrypt session data. Put whatever you want.

Configuring Google for OIDC

Like all Cloud Providers, Google offers a full-fledged Identity Management solution, which may be daunting for newcomers. In this section, I'll only detail the necessary steps required to configure it for OIDC.

On the Cloud Console, create a dedicated project (or use an existing one).

If you didn't do it already, customize the OAuth Consent Screen.

In the project context, navigate APIs & Services | Credentials.

Then, press the + CREATE CREDENTIALS button in the upper menu bar.

Select OAuth Client Id in the scrolling menu.

Fill in the fields:

  • Application type: Web application
  • Name: whatever you want
  • Authorized redirect URIs: <URL>/callback, e.g., http://localhost:9080/callback

URL should be the URL of the web application. Likewise, /callback should match the openid-connect plugin configuration above. Note that Google doesn't allow relative URLs, so if you need to reuse the application in different environments, you need to add the URL of each environment. Click the Create button.

In the Docker Compose configuration above, use the Client ID and Client Secret as OIDC_CLIENTID and OIDC_SECRET. I wrote them down as environment variables in a .env file.

The last missing variable is OIDC_ISSUER: it's accounts.google.com. If you navigate to accounts.google.com/.well-known/openid-…, you'll see all data required by OAuth 2.0 (and more).

At this point, we can start our setup with docker compose up. When we navigate to localhost:9080/, the browser redirects us to the Google authentication page. Since I'm already authenticated, I can choose my ID - and I need one bound to the organization of the project I created above.

Then, I can freely access the resource.

Configuring Azure for OIDC

My colleague Bobur has already described everything you need to do to configure Azure for OIDC.

We only need to change the OIDC parameters:

  • OIDC_CLIENTID
  • OIDC_SECRET
  • OIDC_ISSUER: on Azure, it should look something like login.microsoftonline.com/<TENANT_ID>/v2.0

If you restart Docker Compose with the new parameters, the root page is now protected by Azure login.

Conclusion

Externalizing your authentication process to a third party may be sensible, but you want to avoid binding your infrastructure to its proprietary process. OpenID Connect is an industry standard that allows switching providers easily.

Apache APISIX offers a plugin that integrates OIDC so that you can protect your applications with the latter. There's no reason not to use it, as all dedicated identity providers, such as Okta and Keycloak, are OIDC-compatible.

The complete source code for this post can be found on GitHub:

To go further:

Originally published at A Java Geek on March 5th, 2023

Cossack Labs - Dec 14 '22

Rishita Shaw - Dec 13 '22

Ingo Steinke - Dec 12 '22

item.title: LIAPP 在 2023 年网络安全奖中获奖！ | LIAPP has won an award in Cyber Security Awards 2023!
item.description:
你好！ 这就是 LIAPP 团队！

我们很高兴与您分享一些好消息。 赢得这些奖项证明了 LIAPP 致力于确保一流的应用程序安全性。

我们为我们的应用程序安全性感到自豪，我们相信通过 LIAPP，您开发的应用程序也可以是安全的。
那么，为什么不试试 LIAPP，亲眼看看我们如何帮助您满足您的应用程序安全需求呢？

我们对LIAPP的应用保护系统充满信心！

原文:

   

Hello! This is the LIAPP team!

We are excited to share some great news with you. LIAPP has recently won awards in three different categories Mobile Application Security, Application Security, and Runtime Application Self-Protection, which are all related to app security.

Winning these awards is a testament to LIAPP's commitment to ensuring top-notch app security.

We take pride in our app security and we believe that through LIAPP, your developed app too can be secure.
So why not give LIAPP a try and see for yourself how we can help you with your app security needs?

LIAPP protects apps using its action-based detection system and is currently being used in various industries such as fintech, gaming, and enterprise in multiple countries.

We are confident in LIAPP's app protection system!

LIAPP, we provide the best service possible.

MR. ANDOH FRANCIS - Mar 9

item.title: 让我们继续移动球门柱 | Let's keep moving the goal posts
item.description:
自从我加入科技行业以来，我很高兴看到一种将许多旧思维抛在脑后的演变。

我们远不能庆祝胜利一圈，而且进步的反弹往往会像旧地方一样痛苦，但当旧的传统智慧受到挑战，并且不再被视为智慧时，我很高兴。

破损的管道

不久前，人们很容易将在多样性和包容性方面取得的进展视为我们未来可以实现的目标，因为唯一的问题是“管道问题”——也就是说，如果我们以不同的方式教孩子，我们最终可能改变一些东西。 它没有用，因为它不是孩子在工作场所受到骚扰，没有晋升机会，也不是刻板印象中放弃技术角色。

我认为这个概念很长一段时间都受到了关注，但最终它受到了主流的批判性挑战。 它仍然是一个想法，主要是由坚持旧想法的老头脑抛出的，但是将这些挑战视为我们现在无法实现的事情不再是可以接受的。

移动球门柱

球门柱已经移动，并且必须继续移动。 我们的行业还有太多无形的壁垒，但行业领导者不能再完全躲在平淡的委婉语后面。 下一阶段不是接受小胜利，而是要求大胜利。

科技金融放缓似乎导致了对多元化和包容性的投资放缓——这是不可接受的。 我们知道这是今天的问题，而不是明天的问题，尤其是在艰难时期。

未来必须包括一切，我们必须接受更快的社会进步步伐是必要的。 我们必须能够放弃昨天的想法（比如面对经过足够审查的现实，#shecoded 仍然很酷的想法，这是一个排他性的想法）。

大家编码愉快！



原文:

Since I joined the tech industry, I have been heartened to see an evolution which has left a lot of old type of thinking behind.

We are far from able to celebrate a victory lap, and often the backlash to progress can be as painful as the old place, but I am happy when old conventional wisdom is challenged, and is no longer seen as wisdom at all.

The broken pipeline

Not too long ago, it was easy to dismiss progress on diversity and inclusion as something we can achieve in the future, because the only problem is a "pipeline problem" — that is to say, if we teach the children differently, we might eventually change some things. It didn't work, because it wasn't children being harassed in the workplace, passed over for promotion, or stereotyped into giving up on technical roles.

I think this notion received eye-rolls for a long time, but eventually it received mainstream critical challenges. It is still an idea thrown around, primarily by old heads clinging to old ideas, but it is no longer acceptable to think of these challenges as things we can't achieve right now.

Moving the goalposts

The goal posts have moved, and have to keep moving. Our industry still has too many invisible barriers, but industry leaders can no longer entirely hide behind bland euphemisms. The next phase is not accept small wins, but demand big wins.

A tech financial slowdown seems to have led to a slowdown in investment in diversity and inclusion — and this can't be acceptable. We know this is today's problem, not tomorrow's especially in tough tiimes.

The future must include everything, we must accept that a faster pace of social progress is necessary. We must be able to let go of yesterday's ideas (like the notion that it's still cool to do #shecoded in face of the reality that given enough scrutiny, it was an exclusionary idea).

Happy coding, everyone!

item.title: 如何管理 VSCode 扩展？ | How to manage VSCode extensions?
item.description:
波萨瓦特萨诺卡姆

发表于 3 月 9 日

从文档中，您可以看到可以通过导出来共享您的配置文件。

这意味着，我们可以从要点或文件中为任何开发人员快速设置 VSCode。

因此，每个协作者都将共享相同的环境，您可以使用相同的工具一起工作。 也许，您可以代表您添加更多扩展并继续工作。

一旦暂停，parmcoder 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，parmcoder 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，parmcoder 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 parmcoder 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 parmcoder 的操作：

降低 parmcoder 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 parmcoder 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Possawat Sanorkam

Posted on Mar 9

   

The new update from VSCode is Profiles system.

From the doc, you can see that you can share your profiles by exporting it.

That means, we can quickly set up the VSCode for any dev from a gist or file.

So, every collaborator will share the same environment and you can work together with the same tools. Possibly, you can add more extensions on your behalf and keep working also.

Once suspended, parmcoder will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, parmcoder will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by parmcoder will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If parmcoder is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag parmcoder:

Make all posts by parmcoder less visible

parmcoder consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging parmcoder will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: parmcoder

item.title: AI 正在改变我们编码的方式：AI 驱动开发 (AIDD) | AI Is Changing The Way We Code: AI-Driven Development (AIDD)
item.description:
如今，似乎任何人都可以编写代码。 您可能已经在网上看到大量非开发人员使用 AI 立即生成工作脚本的视频。 实际上似乎有效的代码！ 就像魔术一样。

这有点像魔术。 像 ChatGPT 这样的下一代模型在 2023 年出现并引起了几乎所有人的注意； 即使是非技术人员也对 AI 可以做的所有事情感到惊讶，编码只是其中之一。

不仅如此，它每个月都变得更好、更智能。 改进的速度绝对是惊人的。 似乎一切都将改变。

这是一个 Br-AI-ve 新世界

但是等等……这是否意味着开发人员即将被 AI 取代？

当然，如果奶奶现在可以使用 AI 生成的归并排序算法在 O(log n) 时间内不费吹灰之力地分而治之数据结构，那怎么可能不是这个意思呢？

也许开始在别处寻找工作是明智的。 园艺怎么样？ 管道？ 瑜伽教练的影响力？ 任何我们可以保护自己免受这种突然的人工智能干扰的地方。

好吧，等一下。 在这个新世界里，代码确实可以由人工智能生成。 代码也很棒。 但问题在于：我们如何才能确信这段代码会在不断变化的环境中以良好的方式实际执行它应该执行的操作？ 特别是在由其他人工智能生成的代码组成的软件系统环境中，它必须与之集成。

作为开发人员，我们如何才能利用这些新 AI 伙伴的巨大智能并从中受益，同时确保我们客户的复杂软件系统保持可维护性、良好扩展性和无错误运行？

这就是 AIDD 解决的问题。

在本文及其随附的视频中，我们将探索 AI 驱动开发的八个核心步骤，并了解此工作流程如何有可能增强您作为开发人员的生活。 以及它如何做到这一点，不是通过与 AI 对抗——也不是向 AI 投降，而是通过与它们联手。

人类发展技能的价值

但在我们这样做之前，对于那些可能仍然担心人工智能的兴起以及它可能如何影响他们的工作保障的开发人员来说。

您作为开发人员的技能和创造力对软件构建过程仍然至关重要的确切原因是什么？

作为未来的 AIDD 开发人员，您可以承担许多激动人心的职责，以下只是其中的五项。 有些你会很熟悉，有些是全新的。

  1. 成为对模糊的人类客户需求的广阔空间有深刻而透彻理解的人。

  2. 仔细引导 AI 完成 AIDD 过程。

  3. 创建应用程序的测试金字塔。 从单元到集成再到 E2E 测试。 是的，AI 可以而且会在这里帮助你，但你最终要对测试套件的健康和维护负责。

  4. 修复和修改使用 AIDD 构建的系统。

  5. 使用您作为开发人员的技能来确保系统满足客户要求，同时尊重专业软件构建的许多原则：

  • 正确（相对于越野车、崩溃、无所事事或不执行）
  • 稳定的（相对于脆弱的，或非确定性的）
  • 可读（与不可读或混淆）
  • 可测试（相对于紧耦合）
  • 可扩展（相对于紧密耦合）
  • 可扩展（相对于紧耦合）
  • 灵活（相对于紧耦合）
  • 可重用（相对于紧密耦合）
  • 内聚（与低内聚）
  • 可维护（与非 DRY、无文档、死代码等相比）
  • 性能（与慢）
  • 安全（与不安全）
  • 可用（与令人沮丧的用户体验相比）
  • 可访问（相对于特权用户）
  • 便携式（相对于与特定介质紧密耦合）
  • 最小（相对于过度设计）


这五项能力——处理人类客户及其模糊需求、AI 指导、测试套件创建/维护、修复/修改 AI 创建的系统、高级软件原则的智慧——只是你仍将成为作为开发人员在工作场所很有价值。

在可预见的未来，可以合理地假设其中许多技能不会被 AI 接管。

那么什么是人工智能驱动开发？

它还采用了在实现代码之前编写测试的技术。 在某种程度上，我们可以说 AIDD 就像是 TDD 的未来扩展。

然而，与开发人员负责自己创建单元测试和实现代码的传统 TDD 不同，AIDD 引入了一种新方法，强调深度 AI 团队合作。

使用 AIDD 技术，您不再是一个人工作； 您身边有一位聪明而耐心的 AI 盟友，随时准备在几乎每个开发阶段为您提供帮助。 您的 AI 伴侣可以在幕后工作，而您可以专注于更高级别的开发任务，而无需您承担繁重的工作。

为了演示该技术，让我们首先在一般级别上完成这些步骤。 之后，在一段视频中，我们将通过一个简单而实用的示例来具体说明，我们将在其中构建一个独立的函数来实现某个目标。

通过这种方式，您将开始了解此流程的运作方式，以及它如何真正帮助您为客户提供价值。

在极高的层次上考虑功能； 你想达到什么目的？ 仅从输入→输出的角度考虑。

具体来说，它应该采用多少种参数？ 它们是否应该是可选的？ 使用强类型语言（TypeScript、C#、Haskell），无论您在项目中使用什么，都可以手动为函数的输入和输出编写类型或接口。 始终争取纯函数，除非你别无选择，只能引起副作用。

使用您创建的函数类型，将它们交给 AI 并要求它构造一个模拟函数。 这是一个没有实现的函数； 它只是通过伪造输出来模拟真实的功能。

要求 AI 根据函数类型和您想要实现的目标的描述，为这个尚不存在的函数创建单元测试，数量不限。

仔细检查它创建的测试。 如有必要，手动添加更多，直到您觉得如果这些测试通过，您就会真正对函数的正确性充满信心。

这是执行第 4 步的另一种可能方法：自己编写所有测试，但每个测试用例只使用一个断言。 然后，您可以要求 AI 为每个生成 5、10、20 个类似的断言。

每个独特的测试用例有更多的断言，函数靠运气通过测试的概率就会降低。

这是通过不依赖任何外部状态或环境，采用大量使用纯函数、Promise 或 Maybe 等单子、依赖注入、模拟等技术来实现的。

使用模拟函数运行测试并观察它们急剧失败。

如果大多数测试都没有失败——有些可能会因为运气而通过——返回到第 4 步并重写测试，使其更加全面和包罗万象。

现在终于到了 TDD 和 AIDD 神奇的最后一步：重构。 我们实际上是在这里创建然后改进功能。

给 AI 你在第 2 步中做的类型或类型。或者给它一个简单的口头解释。 甚至您所做的所有单元测试。 并释放它！

使用它创建的功能，并希望看到它们光荣地通过！

如果他们不这样做——这可能会经常发生，至少现在是 2023 年——与 AI 合作以实现通过测试的目标。 你可以：

  • 请 AI 再试一次，提供您可能需要的任何错误消息和想法来帮助它解决问题。

或者：

  • 手动检查该功能，看看您是否可以修复它，或者自己实施。 向 AI 寻求帮助你的想法。

一旦所有测试都通过，重要的是要手动审查功能并评估它是否符合前面提到的专业软件建设精神。 如果没有，可以考虑重构函数。

如果测试在重构过程中开始失败，请继续与 AI 协作，遵循与步骤 7 中相同的方法，直到所有测试再次通过。

全做完了！ 祝贺你自己，庆祝你离为客户带来幸福又近了一步！ 但是，我相信一般来说 AIDD 也适用于其他测试策略。 也就是说，应该承认这些其他测试通常涵盖更复杂的场景，因此需要开发人员进行更多的手动操作和仔细考虑。
最后，让我们看看如何在现实世界中使用 AIDD 的实际示例。

为什么不尝试跟随您自己的 AI 伙伴呢？

如果您确实按照视频进行操作，请稍等片刻，并接受您实际上并没有自己创建该功能的事实。 您甚至没有自己创建所有单元测试！

相反，你就像一个神； 用微妙的推动来引导你的创作——但从不直接干预。

至少对我来说，这是令人兴奋的。

电弧。 在空闲时间，他喜欢哲学、模拟合成器、意识以及与朋友和家人在一起。

金字塔和循环图形的功劳：Jenny Eckerud。
视频中使用的 TS/Jest CodeSandbox模板。



原文:

These days, it appears that anyone can write code. You’ve probably seen plenty of videos online of non-developers using AI to generate working scripts in no time. Code that actually seems to work! It’s like magic.

And it is kind of like magic. These next-generation models like ChatGPT emerged onto the scene in 2023 and blew almost everyone’s minds; even non-technical people were amazed by all the things AI could do, coding being just one of them.

And not only that, it is getting better and smarter by the month. The pace of improvement is absolutely astounding. It seems everything is about to change.

It's a Br-AI-ve New World

But wait… doesn’t that mean developers are about to be replaced by AI?

Surely if grandma can now divide-and-conquer a data structure using an AI-generated merge sort algorithm in O(log n) time without breaking a sweat, how could it not mean exactly that?

Maybe it’d be smart to start looking elsewhere for work. How about Gardening? Plumbing? Yoga instructor influencer? Anywhere where we might shield ourselves from this sudden AI disruption.

Well, hold on just a moment. In this new world, code can indeed be generated by AI. Great code, too. But here’s the kicker: how can we gain confidence that this code will actually do what it is supposed to do, in a good way, and in an ever-changing environment? Especially within the context of a software system consisting of other AI-generated code, with which it is going to have to integrate.

How can we as developers harness and benefit from the vast intelligence of these new AI companions, while also ensuring that our customers’ complex software systems remain maintainable, scale well, and function without bugs?

That is the problem that AIDD solves.

In this article and its accompanied video, we'll explore the eight core steps of AI-Driven Development and see how this workflow could have the potential to supercharge your life as a developer. And how it does so not by fighting against—nor surrendering—to the AI, but by joining forces with them.

The Value Of Human Development Skills

But before we do, for the developers out there who might still be concerned about the rise of Artificial Intelligence and how it might affect their job security.

What exactly are the reasons why your skills and creativity as a developer will remain crucial to the software construction process?

Here are just five of the many exciting responsibilities you can expect as a future AIDD developer. Some you will be familiar with, some are brand new.

  1. Be the one who has a deep and thorough understanding of the vast space of vague human customer requirements.

  2. Carefully guide the AI through the AIDD process.

  3. Create your application’s testing pyramid. From unit to integration to E2E tests. Yes, the AI can and will help you out here, but you are ultimately responsible for the health and maintenance of the test suite.

  4. Repair and modify systems built using AIDD.

  5. Use your skills as a developer to make sure the system fulfils customer requirements while also respecting the many principles of professional software construction:
     

  • Correct (vs buggy, crashing, doing nothing, or not executing)
  • Stable (vs brittle, or non-deterministic)
  • Readable (vs unreadable, or obfuscating)
  • Testable (vs tightly coupled)
  • Scalable (vs tightly coupled)
  • Extensible (vs tightly coupled)
  • Flexible (vs tightly coupled)
  • Reusable (vs tightly coupled)
  • Cohesive (vs low cohesion)
  • Maintainable (vs not DRY, no documentation, dead code, etc)
  • Performant (vs slow)
  • Secure (vs insecure)
  • Usable (vs frustrating user experience)
  • Accessible (vs only for privileged users)
  • Portable (vs tightly coupled to specific medium)
  • Minimal (vs over-engineered)  

 
These five competencies—dealing with human customers and their ambiguous requirements, AI guidance, test suite creation/maintenance, repair/modification of AI-created systems, wisdom of high-level software principles—are just some of the reasons why you will still be valuable in the workplace as a developer.

For the foreseeable future, it is reasonable to assume that many of these skills won’t be taken over by AI.

So What Is AI-Driven Development?

AIDD follows the 'red, green, refactor' cycle, just like Test-Driven Development (TDD). It also employs the technique of writing tests before implementation code. In a way, we can say that AIDD is like a futuristic extension of TDD.

However, unlike in traditional TDD where developers are responsible for creating both unit tests and implementation code on their own, AIDD introduces a new approach, emphasizing deep AI teamwork.

Employing the AIDD technique, you are no longer working alone; you have a smart and patient AI ally by your side, ready to come to your aid at almost every stage of development. Instead of you doing the heavy lifting, your AI companion goes to work behind the scenes while you can focus on higher-level development tasks.

The 8 Steps of AIDD

To demo the technique, let’s first go through the steps at a general level. After that, in a video we’ll go concrete with a simple yet practical example, where we construct an isolated function to achieve some goal.

This way, you will start to get a feel for how this process works, and how it can really empower you in delivering value to your customers.

1: Set the goal

Think about the function at an extremely high level; what are you trying to achieve? Consider it only in terms of Input→Output.

☝🏻 Reflect on the function’s API, the ergonomics of actually using the function. Specifically, how many and what kind of arguments should it take? Should they be optional or not? Etc.

2: Formulate the abstract type

Use a strongly-typed language—TypeScript, C#, Haskell—whatever you are using in your project, to manually write the type or interface for the function's input and the output. Strive always for pure functions, unless you have absolutely no choice but to cause side effects.

☝🏻 If you need help in this or any other step, as always—ask the AI.

3: Construct mock functionality

Using the function type you made, give them to the AI and ask it to construct a mock function. This is a function without an implementation; it simply simulates a real function by faking the output.

4: Write tests

Ask the AI to create unit tests for this yet-non-existent-function, as many as you need, based on the function type and a description of what you are trying to achieve.

Carefully review the tests it creates. If necessary, add more manually, until you get the feeling that if these tests pass, you will actually feel confident in the function’s correctness.

Here's another possible way to do step 4: write all the tests yourself, but with only one assertion for each test case. Then, you can ask the AI to generate 5, 10, 20 more similar assertions for each.

With more assertions per unique test case, the probability of the function passing the test by sheer luck decreases.

☝🏻 The unit tests should be lightning fast. This is achieved by not relying on any external state or circumstance, employing techniques like the heavy use of pure functions, monads like Promise or Maybe, dependency injection, mocking, etc.

5: Run tests and expect failure ❌

Run the tests using the mocked function and watch them dramatically fail.

If the majority of the tests are not failing—some probably will pass due to luck—return to step 4 and rewrite the tests to be more comprehensive and all-encompassing.

6: Create concrete implementation

Now finally, it’s time for the magical last step of TDD and AIDD: the refactoring. It is here we actually create and then improve on the function.

Give the AI the types or types you made in step 2. Or give it a simple verbal explanation. Or even all the unit tests you made. And unleash it!

7: Run tests and expect success ✅

Use the function it creates and, hopefully, watch them gloriously pass!

If they don't—which will probably happen quite frequently, at least now in 2023—collaborate with the AI to achieve the goal of the tests passing. You could:

  • Ask the AI to try again, supplying any error messages and ideas you might have to help it out.

Or:

  • Manually review the function and see if you can fix it, or implement it yourself. Ask the AI for ideas to help you out.

8: Refactor

Once all the tests have passed, it's important to review the function manually and assess whether it aligns with the spirit of professional software construction mentioned earlier. If not, maybe consider refactoring the function.

☝🏻 Again, if you're still not sure why you won't be replaced by AI anytime soon, the step above is one of the main reasons.

If the tests start to fail during the refactoring process, keep collaborating with the AI, following the same approach as in step 7, until all the tests pass again.

(Optional Final Step): Celebrate!

All done! Congratulate yourself and celebrate the fact that you are now one step closer to delivering happiness to your customer! 🎉

☝🏻 In this article we are focusing on unit testing. However, I believe that in general AIDD is applicable to the other testing strategies as well. That said, it should be acknowledged that these other tests typically cover more complex scenarios and therefore require further manual effort and careful thought from the developer.
☝🏻 Although the principles of TDD and AIDD are close to identical, it's not until you start developing that you begin to see the significant differences between the two approaches.

AI-Driven Development in the Wild

Finally, let’s see an actual example of how AIDD can be used in the real world.

Why not attempt to follow along with your own AI companion?

If you did follow along with the video, just pause for just a moment and take in the fact you didn’t actually create that function yourself. You didn’t even create all the unit tests yourself!

Instead, you were like a god; guiding your creation with subtle nudges—yet never directly intervening.

That, at least to me, is mind-blowing.

Dawid Dahl is a full-stack developer at UMAIN | ARC. In his free time, he enjoys philosophy, analog synthesizers, consciousness, and being with friends and family.

Credit for pyramid and cycle graphics: Jenny Eckerud.
TS/Jest CodeSandbox template used in the video.
item.author: dawiddahl

item.title: 建立电子商务商店：使用 Solidjs 和 Medusa 的分步指南 | Building an E-commerce Store: A Step-by-Step Guide with Solidjs and Medusa
item.description:
鲁本09

发布于 3 月 9 日 • 最初发布于reuben09.hashnode.dev

人们可以使用电子商务商店在线买卖有形（实物）商品、数字产品或服务。 您将使用 Solid（作为店面）和 Medusa（作为后端）构建一个电子商务商店。

通过创建此应用程序，您将学习：

  1. 如何设置 Medusa 服务器和 Medusa 管理员。

  2. 如何使用 Solid 创建电子商务店面。

  3. 如何将 Solid 与 Medusa 集成

  4. 如何使用<For>组件遍历对象数组。

  5. 如何使用 Solid Router 创建客户端路由。

  6. 如何使用 Material UI 创建漂亮的 UI

以及更多...

什么是美杜莎？

美杜莎由三部分组成：

  • 无头后端。

  • 管理仪表板。

  • 店面。

什么是固体？

借助 Solid，您可以使用现有的 HTML 和 JavaScript 知识来构建可在整个应用程序中重复使用的组件。 让我们使用 Solid 创建一个很酷的电子商务网站。

美杜莎入门

在本文的这一部分，我将向您展示如何在本地计算机上设置 Medusa 服务器。

第 1 步：安装 Medusa CLI 工具

要使用 Medusa，您必须先安装 CLI 工具，该工具用于创建新的 Medusa 服务器。

使用npm或yarn ，我们可以安装 Medusa CLI，但本教程将使用npm 。 打开您选择的任何终端并运行以下命令：

 npm install @medusajs/medusa-cli -g

第 2 步：创建新的 Medusa Store Server

商店服务器是存放商店所有逻辑和数据的主要组件。

该服务器将包括与您商店结帐工作流程相关的所有功能。 包括购物车管理、运输、运输、支付提供商、用户管理和其他功能。

运行以下命令以创建新的美杜莎商店服务器：

 medusa new my-medusa-store --seed

上述命令的注意事项：

  • 包括商店、管理员帐户、区域和具有变体的产品。

第 3 步：测试 Medusa Store 服务器

要测试 Medusa 商店服务器，请 cd 进入my-medusa-store ，然后运行以下命令：

在浏览器中导航到localhost:9000/store/products/ ，应该会出现以下内容：

第 4 步：设置 Medusa Admin

它为您提供了一种创建新产品、编辑产品、删除产品、查看订单等的方法。

要安装管理员，首先克隆管理员 Github 存储库：

 git clone https://github.com/medusajs/admin medusa-admin

接下来，进入medusa-admin目录并使用 npm 安装依赖项：

第 5 步：测试 Medusa Admin

要运行 Medusa 管理员，请输入以下命令：

现在在浏览器上导航到localhost:7000以查看管理页面：

电子邮件是admin@medusa-test.com密码是supersecret 。

注意：在测试 Medusa 管理员之前，您需要确保 Medusa 服务器正在运行。

使用 Solid 构建店面

在本文的这一部分，我将演示如何使用 Solidjs 为您的电子商务应用程序构建店面。

第 1 步：创建一个新的 Solid App

在您的终端中运行以下命令以创建一个新的 Solid 应用程序：

 npx degit solidjs/templates/js solid-app

接下来， cd进入solid-app并安装其依赖项：

安装完成后，启动本地开发服务器：

第 2 步：安装所有必需的依赖项

要更快地构建电子商务应用程序，您将需要一些依赖项。

在Material UI的帮助下，我们将能够轻松创建令人惊叹的 UI。 运行以下命令为 Solid 安装 Material UI：

 npm install @suid/material @suid/icons-material

要安装 Solid Router，请运行以下命令：

运行以下命令安装 Medusa JS 客户端：

 npm install @medusajs/medusa-js

第 3 步：将 Solid 与 Medusa 集成

在 solid-app 项目的根目录下，创建一个.env文件并向其中添加以下内容：

 VITE_baseUrl=http://localhost:9000

您必须配置 Vite，因为 Medusa 默认只接受端口8000上的店面访问，而 Vite 的默认端口是5173 。

导航到vite.config.json并将内容替换为以下内容：

 import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; import solidPlugin from 'vite-plugin-solid'; //https://vitejs.dev/config/ export default defineConfig({ plugins: [solidPlugin()], server: { port: 8000, }, build: { target: 'esnext', }, });

重新启动开发服务器后，打开一个新的浏览器选项卡并导航到http://localhost:8000 。

注意：如果 Vite 未配置为使用所需的端口号8000 ，则您的店面向服务器发出的任何请求都将被 Medusa 拒绝。

接下来，创建一个实用程序，使您能够在整个应用程序中重用 Medusa JS 客户端实例。

新建一个文件src/utils/client.js并添加以下内容：

 import Medusa from "@medusajs/medusa-js" const medusaClient = new Medusa({ baseUrl: import.meta.env.VITE_MEDUSA_SERVER_URL }) export { medusaClient }

第 4 步：构建店面组件

在本文的这一部分，我们将为我们的应用构建店面组件。 我们要创建的组件之一是：

  • 然后在components文件夹中，创建一个新的Header.jsx文件并添加以下内容：
 //Header.jsx import { A } from "@solidjs/router"; import { Box } from "@suid/material"; import { Typography, IconButton, AppBar, Toolbar } from "@suid/material"; import ShoppingCartIcon from "@suid/icons-material/ShoppingCart"; const Header = () => { const cartCount = localStorage.getItem("cartCount") ?? 0; return ( <Box> <AppBar position="fixed" color="primary"> <Toolbar> <Box sx={{ display: "flex", width: "100%", alignItems: "center", flexDirection: { lg: "row", md: "column", sm: "column", xs: "row", }, justifyContent: "space-between", }} > <Typography variant="h6" component="h6" sx={{ marginBottom: { lg: "0", md: "0.7rem", sm: "0.7rem", xs: "0" }, }} > Reuben09 </Typography> </Box> <Box sx={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", marginBottom: { lg: "0", md: "0.7rem", sm: "0.7rem", xs: "0" }, }} > <A href="/"> <Typography sx={{ marginRight: "1rem", fontSize: "1.1rem", cursor: "pointer", }} > Home </Typography> </A> <A href="/products"> <Typography sx={{ marginRight: "1rem", fontSize: "1.1rem", cursor: "pointer", }} > Products </Typography> </A> <IconButton> <ShoppingCartIcon sx={{ color: "#fff" }} /> </IconButton> <p>{cartCount}</p> </Box> </Toolbar> </AppBar> </Box> ); }; export default Header;

  • 在components文件夹中，创建一个新文件并将其命名为ProductList.jsx 。 打开新创建的ProductList.jsx文件并添加以下内容：

 //ProductList.jsx import { createEffect, createSignal, For } from "solid-js"; import { A } from "@solidjs/router"; import { Box, Typography, Link } from "@suid/material"; import { medusaClient } from "../utils/client.js"; function ProductList() { const [products, setProducts] = createSignal([]); createEffect(() => { const fetchProducts = async () => { const res = await medusaClient.products.list(); setProducts(res.products); }; fetchProducts(); }); return ( <> <Box sx={{ marginTop: "2rem", paddingLeft: { lg: "4rem", xs: "1.5rem" }, paddingRight: { lg: "4rem", xs: "1.5rem" }, }} component="section" > <Typography sx={{ textAlign: "center", fontSize: "1.5rem", marginBottom: "2rem", }} > Featured Products </Typography> <Box sx={{ margin: "0 2rem" }}> <Box sx={{ display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr", gridGap: "2rem", }} > <For each={products()}> {(product) => ( <Box component="div" sx={{ backgroundColor: "rgb(231, 231, 231)", height: "350px", padding: "0.5rem", }} > <Box component="div" sx={{ height: "12rem" }}> <Box component="img" src={product?.thumbnail} sx={{ height: "12rem", width: "100%", objectFit: "cover", }} /> </Box> <Typography sx={{ padding: "1rem 0", textAlign: "center", fontWeight: "600", }} > {product?.title} </Typography> <Typography sx={{ textAlign: "center" }}> € {product?.variants[0].prices[0].amount / 100} </Typography> <Box sx={{ textAlign: "center" }}> <Link underline="always"> <A href={`/products/${product.id}`}>See product</A> </Link> </Box> </Box> )} </For> </Box> </Box> </Box> </> ); } export default ProductList;
  • 第 5 步：创建店面页面

    从这里开始就变得简单了，您需要做的就是将您在上一节中创建的组件导入到这些页面中：

    • 主页：在src目录下新建一个文件夹，命名为pages 。 然后在pages文件夹中，创建一个新的Home.jsx文件并添加以下内容：
     import { Box, Typography, IconButton, Button } from "@suid/material"; import ProductList from '../component/ProductList' import Footer from '../component/Footer' function Home (){ return <div> <Box sx={{ backgroundImage: "url(https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1552346154-21d32810aba3?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=870&q=80)", width: "100%", height: "80vh", backgroundSize: "cover", backgroundPosition: "center", backgroundRepeat: "no-repeat", marginBottom: "5rem" }} > <Box sx={{ width: "100%", height: "80vh", backgroundColor: "rgba(25, 118, 210, 0.2)", display: "flex", flexDirection: "column", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center" }} > <Typography sx={{ fontSize: "3rem", color: "#ffffff", }} > Reuben09 </Typography> <Typography sx={{ fontSize: "3rem", color: "#ffffff", }} > Sneakers </Typography> <Typography sx={{textAlign: "center", color: "#ffffff" }}> Users of the highest converting shopify theme deserve a lifestyle to match! </Typography> <Box sx={{marginTop: "1rem" }}> <IconButton> <Button variant="contained" color="primary" sx={{marginRight: "0.5rem" }} > Shop now </Button> </IconButton> <IconButton> <Button variant="contained" color="primary" sx={{marginLeft: "0.5rem" }} > Learn more </Button> </IconButton> </Box> </Box> </Box> <Box component="div" sx={{marginBottom: "2rem" }}> <ProductList /> </Box> <Box section="footer"> <Footer /> </Box> </div> } export default Home;
    • 产品页面：同样在pages文件夹中，新建一个Products.jsx文件并添加以下内容：
     import ProductList from '../component/ProductList' import Footer from '../component/Footer' function Products (){ return <div> <ProductList /> <Footer /> </div> } export default Products;

    • 它还将允许用户将产品添加到他们的购物车

      在pages文件夹中新建一个SingleProduct.jsx文件并粘贴以下内容：

     import { useParams } from "@solidjs/router"; import { createEffect, createSignal } from 'solid-js' import {Container, Box, Grid, IconButton, Button, Typography} from '@suid/material'; import Footer from '../component/Footer' import { medusaClient } from '../utils/client.js' const addProduct = async (cartId, product) => { const { cart } = await medusaClient.carts.lineItems.create(cartId, { variant_id: product()?.variants[0].id, quantity: 1 }) console.log(cart); localStorage.setItem('cartCount', cart.items.length) setTimeout(window.location.reload(), 5000) } function SingleProduct (){ const [productItem, setProductItem] = createSignal(); const [regionId, setRegionId] = createSignal(""); const params = useParams(); const productId = params.productId; createEffect(()=> { const fetchSingleProduct = async () => { const results = await medusaClient.products.retrieve(productId); setProductItem(results.product) } const fetchRegions = async () => { const results = await medusaClient.regions.list() setRegionId(results.regions[1].id) } fetchSingleProduct() fetchRegions() }) const handleAddToCart = async () => { const cartId = localStorage.getItem('cartId'); if (cartId) { addProduct(cartId, productItem) } else { const { cart } = await medusaClient.carts.create({ region_id: regionId }) localStorage.setItem('cartId', cart.id); addProduct(cart.id, productItem) } } return <> <Box sx={{marginTop: "7rem"}}> <Container maxWidth="sm"> <Grid container spacing={2}> <Grid item xs={6}> <Box> <img sx={{width:"500px"}} alt={productItem()?.title} src={productItem()?.thumbnail} /> </Box> </Grid> <Grid item xs={6}> <Box sx={{marginTop: "2rem", display: "flex", justifyContent: "flex-start", alignItems: "flex-start", flexDirection: "column"}}> <Typography variant="h5" mb={2}>{productItem()?.title}</Typography> <Typography mb={2}>€ {productItem()?.variants[0].prices[0].amount / 100 }</Typography> <Typography mb={2}>{productItem()?.description}</Typography> <IconButton> <Button onClick={handleAddToCart} variant="contained" color="primary"> Add to cart </Button> </IconButton> </Box> </Grid> </Grid> </Container> </Box> <Footer /> </> } export default SingleProduct;

    第 6 步：注册店面页面：

    在本文的这一部分，我们将使用 Solid Router 在我们的应用程序中启用客户端路由。

    打开index.jsx并将其替换为以下内容：

     /* @refresh reload */ import { render } from 'solid-js/web'; import { Router } from "@solidjs/router"; import './index.css'; import App from './App'; const root = document.getElementById('root'); if (import.meta.env.DEV && !(root instanceof HTMLElement)) { throw new Error( 'Root element not found. Did you forget to add it to your index.html? Or maybe the id attribute got mispelled?', ); } render(() => <Router> <App /> </Router>, root);

    就是这样！ 您现在可以注册这三个页面。 打开App.jsx并将其替换为以下内容：

     import { Routes, Route } from "@solidjs/router" import { lazy } from "solid-js"; import Header from './component/Header' const Products = lazy(() => import("./pages/Products")); const Home = lazy(() => import("./pages/Home")); const SingleProduct = lazy(() => import("./pages/SingleProduct")); function App() { return ( <div> <Header /> <Routes> <Route path="/" component={Home} /> <Route path="/products" component={Products} /> <Route path="/products/:productId" component={SingleProduct} /> </Routes> </div> ); } export default App;

    第 7 步：测试店面

    重新启动您的服务器并在http://localhost:8000打开一个新的浏览器选项卡，这是您的浏览器应该显示的内容：

    主页：

    产品页面：在您的导航栏上，单击产品以进入产品页面：

    单品页面：点击任一产品进入单品页面：

    结论

    在本文中介绍了 Medusa 和 Solid 之后，我展示了如何使用 Medusa（作为后端）和 Solid（作为店面）从头开始创建一个电子商务网站。

    参考

    美杜莎文档 - docs.medusajs.com/introduction

    固体路由器 - github.com/solidjs/solid-router

    材质 UI - suid.io/getting-started/usage

一旦暂停，reuben09 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，reuben09 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，reuben09 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 reuben09 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 reuben09 的方法：

降低 reuben09 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 reuben09 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Reuben09

Posted on Mar 9 • Originally published at reuben09.hashnode.dev

   

The term “e-commerce” simply refers to the online sale of goods and services.

People can use an e-commerce store to buy and sell tangible (physical) goods, digital products, or services online. You will build an e-commerce store using Solid(as the storefront) and Medusa(as the backend).

By creating this app, you will learn:

  1. How to set up the Medusa server and Medusa admin.

  2. How to Create an e-commerce storefront using Solid.

  3. How to Integrate Solid with Medusa

  4. How to loop over an array of objects using the <For> component.

  5. How to create client-side routing using Solid Router.

  6. How to create beautiful UIs using Material UI

and much more...

What is Medusa?

Medusa is an open-source composable e-commerce engine for developers who want to take ownership of their e-commerce stack. Medusa is made up of three parts:

  • The headless backend.

  • Admin dashboard.

  • Storefront.

Medusa is a versatile Javascript e-commerce platform that enables developers to take advantage of its features and capabilities regardless of the framework they use to build their web applications.

What is Solid?

Solid is a blazing-fast javascript framework that dodges virtual dom manipulation. Solid is a JavaScript framework for making interactive web applications.

With Solid, you can use your existing HTML and JavaScript knowledge to build components that can be reused throughout your app. Solid provides the tools to enhance your components with reactivity: declarative JavaScript code that links the user interface with the data that it uses and creates.

Let's create a cool e-commerce website using Solid.

Getting Started with Medusa

In this portion of this article, I am going to show you how to set up the Medusa server on your local machine.

Step 1: Install the Medusa CLI Tool

Medusa CLI is a tool that lets you run important commands while working with Medusa.

To use Medusa, you must first install the CLI tool, which is used to create a new Medusa server.

Using either npm or yarn, we can install Medusa CLI, but this tutorial will use npm. Open any terminal of your choice and run the following command:

npm install @medusajs/medusa-cli -g

Step 2: Create a new Medusa Store Server

The store server is the main component that houses all of the store's logic and data.

This server will include all the functionalities related to your store's checkout workflow. Cart management, shipping, shipping, payment providers, user management, and other features are included.

Run the command below to create your new Medusa store server:

medusa new my-medusa-store --seed

Things to note from the preceding command:

  • my-medusa-store represents the project's name, which you can change to whatever you want.

  • The --seed command seeds your database with some sample data to get you started. Including a store, an administrator account, a region and products with variants.

Step 3: Test the Medusa Store Server

To test the Medusa store server, cd into my-medusa-store, and run the command below:

Navigate to localhost:9000/store/products/ in your browser, and the following should appear:

Step 4: Set up Medusa Admin

The Medusa admin dashboard allows us to perform all kinds of actions on our system data. It provides a way for you to create new products, edit your products, delete your products, view orders and so on.

The Medusa admin is connected to the Medusa store server, so make sure that you have successfully installed and tested the Medusa store server first before proceeding with the admin.

To install the admin, start by cloning the admin Github repository:

git clone https://github.com/medusajs/admin medusa-admin

Next, cd into the medusa-admin directory and install the dependencies using npm:

Step 5: Test the Medusa Admin

To run the Medusa admin, type in the following command:

Now navigate to localhost:7000 on your browser to view the admin page:

The email is admin@medusa-test.com and the password is supersecret.

Note: Before you can test the Medusa admin, you need to make sure the Medusa server is running.

Building the Storefront with Solid

In this section of the article, I'll demonstrate how to use Solidjs to build the storefront for your e-commerce application.

Step 1: Create a new Solid App

Run the following command in your terminal to create a new Solid app:

npx degit solidjs/templates/js solid-app

Next, cd into solid-app and install its dependencies:

Once the installation is complete, start your local development server:

Step 2: Install all Necessary Dependencies

To build the e-commerce app more quickly, you will need some dependencies.

We will be able to easily create stunning UIs with the help of Material UI. Run the following command to install Material UI for Solid:

npm install @suid/material @suid/icons-material

Solid Router is a universal router for Solidjs that works whether you are rendering on the client or on the server. To install Solid Router run the command below:

Medusa JS Client is an SDK that provides easy an way to access the Medusa API. Run the following command to install the Medusa JS Client:

npm install @medusajs/medusa-js

Step 3: Integrate Solid with Medusa

At the root of your solid-app project, create a .env file and add the following content to it:

VITE_baseUrl=http://localhost:9000

baseUrl environment variable is the URL of your Medusa server.

Medusa uses Cross-Origin Resource Sharing(CORS) to only allow specific origins to access the server.

You must configure Vite because Medusa by default only accepts access to storefronts on port 8000, while Vite's default port is 5173.

Navigate into vite.config.json and replace the content with the following:

import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import solidPlugin from 'vite-plugin-solid'; //https://vitejs.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({ plugins: [solidPlugin()], server: { port: 8000, }, build: { target: 'esnext', },
});

Open a new browser tab and navigate to http://localhost:8000 after restarting your development server.

Note: Any request your storefront makes to the server will be denied by Medusa if Vite is not configured to use the required port number, 8000.

Next, create a utility that will enable the reuse of the Medusa JS Client instance throughout your application.

Create a new file src/utils/client.js and add the following content:

import Medusa from "@medusajs/medusa-js" const medusaClient = new Medusa({ baseUrl: import.meta.env.VITE_MEDUSA_SERVER_URL }) export { medusaClient }

Step 4: Building the Storefront Components

In this section of this article, we will build the storefront component for our app. One of the components we are going to create is the:

  • Header Component: Make a new folder in the src directory and call it components. Then within the components folder, make a new Header.jsx file and add the following content: 
//Header.jsx
import { A } from "@solidjs/router";
import { Box } from "@suid/material";
import { Typography, IconButton, AppBar, Toolbar } from "@suid/material";
import ShoppingCartIcon from "@suid/icons-material/ShoppingCart"; const Header = () => { const cartCount = localStorage.getItem("cartCount") ?? 0; return ( <Box> <AppBar position="fixed" color="primary"> <Toolbar> <Box sx={{ display: "flex", width: "100%", alignItems: "center", flexDirection: { lg: "row", md: "column", sm: "column", xs: "row", }, justifyContent: "space-between", }} > <Typography variant="h6" component="h6" sx={{ marginBottom: { lg: "0", md: "0.7rem", sm: "0.7rem", xs: "0" }, }} > Reuben09 </Typography>
 </Box>
 <Box sx={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", marginBottom: { lg: "0", md: "0.7rem", sm: "0.7rem", xs: "0" }, }} > <A href="/"> <Typography sx={{ marginRight: "1rem", fontSize: "1.1rem", cursor: "pointer", }} > Home </Typography>
 </A>
 <A href="/products"> <Typography sx={{ marginRight: "1rem", fontSize: "1.1rem", cursor: "pointer", }} > Products </Typography>
 </A>
 <IconButton> <ShoppingCartIcon sx={{ color: "#fff" }} />
 </IconButton>
 <p>{cartCount}</p>
 </Box>
 </Toolbar>
 </AppBar>
 </Box>
 );
}; export default Header;

  • ProductList Component: This component will display lists of products fetched from the Medusa server using the medusaClient utility function, using Solid Router, the component will also create links to individual products.

    Inside the components folder, make a new file and call it ProductList.jsx. Open the newly created ProductList.jsx file and add the following content:

//ProductList.jsx
import { createEffect, createSignal, For } from "solid-js";
import { A } from "@solidjs/router";
import { Box, Typography, Link } from "@suid/material";
import { medusaClient } from "../utils/client.js"; function ProductList() { const [products, setProducts] = createSignal([]); createEffect(() => { const fetchProducts = async () => { const res = await medusaClient.products.list(); setProducts(res.products); }; fetchProducts(); }); return ( <> <Box sx={{ marginTop: "2rem", paddingLeft: { lg: "4rem", xs: "1.5rem" }, paddingRight: { lg: "4rem", xs: "1.5rem" }, }} component="section" > <Typography sx={{ textAlign: "center", fontSize: "1.5rem", marginBottom: "2rem", }} > Featured Products </Typography>
 <Box sx={{ margin: "0 2rem" }}> <Box sx={{ display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr", gridGap: "2rem", }} > <For each={products()}> {(product) => ( <Box component="div" sx={{ backgroundColor: "rgb(231, 231, 231)", height: "350px", padding: "0.5rem", }} > <Box component="div" sx={{ height: "12rem" }}> <Box component="img" src={product?.thumbnail} sx={{ height: "12rem", width: "100%", objectFit: "cover", }} />
 </Box>
 <Typography sx={{ padding: "1rem 0", textAlign: "center", fontWeight: "600", }} > {product?.title} </Typography>
 <Typography sx={{ textAlign: "center" }}> € {product?.variants[0].prices[0].amount / 100} </Typography>
 <Box sx={{ textAlign: "center" }}> <Link underline="always"> <A href={`/products/${product.id}`}>See product</A>
 </Link>
 </Box>
 </Box>
 )} </For>
 </Box>
 </Box>
 </Box>
 </>
 );
} export default ProductList;
  • Newsletter component: In the components folder create a new NewsLetter.jsx file and add the following content: 
import { Grid, Box, Typography, Input, IconButton, Button } from "@suid/material";
function NewsLetter() { return ( <Box component="section" sx={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", flexDirection: "column", paddingLeft: { lg: "4rem", xs: "2rem" }, paddingRight: { lg: "4rem", xs: "2rem" } }} > <Box component="form"> <Typography variant="h2" mt={10} sx={{ textAlign: "center", fontSize: "1.5rem", marginBottom: "0.3rem", }} > {" "} Subscribe to our newsletter </Typography>
 <Typography sx={{textAlign: "center", marginBottom: "1rem" }}> Get 10% off your first purchase and stay on top of the latest in Debutify, it's win-win-WIN! </Typography> <Box sx={{marginBottom: "2rem" }}> <Input placeholder="First Name" sx={{textAlign: "center", width: "100%" }} /> </Box> <Box sx={{marginBottom: "2rem" }}> <Input placeholder="Your Email" sx={{textAlign: "center", width: "100%" }} /> </Box> <Box sx={{textAlign: "center", width: "100%", marginBottom: "1rem" }}> <IconButton> <Button variant="contained" color="primary"> Subscribe </Button> </IconButton> </Box> </Box> </Box> ); } export default NewsLetter;
  • Footer component: Also in the components folder create a new Footer.jsx file and add this content: 
import { Box, Typography } from "@suid/material";
import NewsLetter from "./NewsLetter"; function Footer (){ return ( <> <Box component="section" sx={{ marginBottom: "2rem" }}> <NewsLetter /> </Box>
 <Box component="div" sx={{textAlign: "center", backgroundColor: "#1976D2", padding: "1rem" }} color="primary" > <Box component="a" sx={{textAlign: "center", color: "#fff" }} href="https://github.com/Reuben09" > created by Reuben09 </Box>
 </Box>
 </>
 )
} export default Footer;

Step 5: Create the Storefront Pages

From here it gets easy, all you need to do is import the components that you have created in the previous section into these pages:

  • Home page: Make a new folder in the src directory and call it pages. Then within the pages folder, create a new Home.jsx file and add the following content: 
import { Box, Typography, IconButton, Button } from "@suid/material";
import ProductList from '../component/ProductList'
import Footer from '../component/Footer' function Home (){ return <div> <Box sx={{ backgroundImage: "url(https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1552346154-21d32810aba3?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=870&q=80)", width: "100%", height: "80vh", backgroundSize: "cover", backgroundPosition: "center", backgroundRepeat: "no-repeat", marginBottom: "5rem" }} > <Box sx={{ width: "100%", height: "80vh", backgroundColor: "rgba(25, 118, 210, 0.2)", display: "flex", flexDirection: "column", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center" }} > <Typography sx={{ fontSize: "3rem", color: "#ffffff", }} > Reuben09 </Typography>
 <Typography sx={{ fontSize: "3rem", color: "#ffffff", }} > Sneakers </Typography>
 <Typography sx={{textAlign: "center", color: "#ffffff" }}> Users of the highest converting shopify theme deserve a lifestyle to match! </Typography>
 <Box sx={{marginTop: "1rem" }}> <IconButton> <Button variant="contained" color="primary" sx={{marginRight: "0.5rem" }} > Shop now </Button>
 </IconButton>
 <IconButton> <Button variant="contained" color="primary" sx={{marginLeft: "0.5rem" }} > Learn more </Button>
 </IconButton>
 </Box>
 </Box>
 </Box>
 <Box component="div" sx={{marginBottom: "2rem" }}> <ProductList /> </Box>
 <Box section="footer"> <Footer /> </Box>
 </div>
} export default Home;
  • Products page: Also in the pages folder, make a new Products.jsx file and add the following content: 
import ProductList from '../component/ProductList'
import Footer from '../component/Footer' function Products (){ return <div> <ProductList /> <Footer /> </div>
} export default Products;

  • SingleProduct page: This page is where we will fetch product items based on their ids and display their details. It will also allow users to add the product to their cart

    Make a new SingleProduct.jsx file in the pages folder and paste in the following content:

import { useParams } from "@solidjs/router";
import { createEffect, createSignal } from 'solid-js'
import {Container, Box, Grid, IconButton, Button, Typography} from '@suid/material';
import Footer from '../component/Footer'
import { medusaClient } from '../utils/client.js' const addProduct = async (cartId, product) => { const { cart } = await medusaClient.carts.lineItems.create(cartId, { variant_id: product()?.variants[0].id, quantity: 1 }) console.log(cart); localStorage.setItem('cartCount', cart.items.length) setTimeout(window.location.reload(), 5000)
} function SingleProduct (){ const [productItem, setProductItem] = createSignal(); const [regionId, setRegionId] = createSignal(""); const params = useParams(); const productId = params.productId; createEffect(()=> { const fetchSingleProduct = async () => { const results = await medusaClient.products.retrieve(productId); setProductItem(results.product) } const fetchRegions = async () => { const results = await medusaClient.regions.list() setRegionId(results.regions[1].id) } fetchSingleProduct() fetchRegions() }) const handleAddToCart = async () => { const cartId = localStorage.getItem('cartId'); if (cartId) { addProduct(cartId, productItem) } else { const { cart } = await medusaClient.carts.create({ region_id: regionId }) localStorage.setItem('cartId', cart.id); addProduct(cart.id, productItem) } } return <> <Box sx={{marginTop: "7rem"}}> <Container maxWidth="sm"> <Grid container spacing={2}> <Grid item xs={6}> <Box> <img sx={{width:"500px"}} alt={productItem()?.title} src={productItem()?.thumbnail} />
 </Box>
 </Grid>
 <Grid item xs={6}> <Box sx={{marginTop: "2rem", display: "flex", justifyContent: "flex-start", alignItems: "flex-start", flexDirection: "column"}}> <Typography variant="h5" mb={2}>{productItem()?.title}</Typography>
 <Typography mb={2}>€ {productItem()?.variants[0].prices[0].amount / 100 }</Typography>
 <Typography mb={2}>{productItem()?.description}</Typography>
 <IconButton> <Button onClick={handleAddToCart} variant="contained" color="primary"> Add to cart </Button>
 </IconButton>
 </Box>
 </Grid>
 </Grid>
 </Container>
 </Box>
 <Footer /> </>
} export default SingleProduct;

Step 6: Register the Storefront Pages:

In this section of this article, we are going to enable client-side routing in our application using Solid Router.

index.jsx is our entry point, so we will import Router and wrap the App component within it.

Open index.jsx and replace it with the following content:

/* @refresh reload */
import { render } from 'solid-js/web';
import { Router } from "@solidjs/router";
import './index.css';
import App from './App'; const root = document.getElementById('root'); if (import.meta.env.DEV && !(root instanceof HTMLElement)) { throw new Error( 'Root element not found. Did you forget to add it to your index.html? Or maybe the id attribute got mispelled?', );
} render(() => <Router>
<App />
</Router>, root);

That's it! you can now register the three pages. Open App.jsx and replace it with the following content:

import { Routes, Route } from "@solidjs/router"
import { lazy } from "solid-js";
import Header from './component/Header'
const Products = lazy(() => import("./pages/Products"));
const Home = lazy(() => import("./pages/Home"));
const SingleProduct = lazy(() => import("./pages/SingleProduct")); function App() { return ( <div> <Header /> <Routes> <Route path="/" component={Home} />
 <Route path="/products" component={Products} />
 <Route path="/products/:productId" component={SingleProduct} />
 </Routes>
 </div>
 );
} export default App;

Step 7: Test the Storefront

Restart your server and open a new browser tab at http://localhost:8000 and this is what your browser should display:

Home page:

Products page: On your navbar, click on products to get to the products page:

SingleProducts page: Click on any of the products to get to the single product page:

Conclusion

After introducing Medusa and Solid in this article, I showed how to create an e-commerce website using Medusa (as the backend) and Solid (as the storefront) from scratch.

Reference

Medusa docs - docs.medusajs.com/introduction

Solid Router - github.com/solidjs/solid-router

Material UI - suid.io/getting-started/usage

[ดู-] ขุนพันธ์ 3 (2566) เต็มเรื่อง HD พากย์ไทย -ออนไลน์ - Mar 9

Shivam Vishvakarma - Mar 9

item.title: 我将循环从 100K 减少到 1K | I reduced loop from 100K to 1K
item.description:
在处理项目时，我想为我拥有的电子邮件 ID 数组返回名称数组。

 const users = [ {name: 'John', email: 'john@example.com'}, {name: 'Jane', email: 'jane@example.com'}, {name: 'Bob', email: 'bob@example.com'} ]; function getNames(emailIds) { return emailIds.map((emailId)=>{ const user = users.find((user)=>{ return user.email === emailId }); return user?.name || emailId }) }

如您所见，上面的代码，时间复杂度为O(n*m) 。 换句话说，假设emailIds数组的大小为 100 而users数组的大小为 1000 那么上面的代码可能会运行1000*100 = 100000 ，所以为了减少迭代

function getNames(emailIds) { const emailMap= {}; users.forEach((user)=>{ emailMap[user.email]=user.name }); return emailIds.map((emailId)=>{ return emailMap[emailId] || emailId }) }

该函数首先初始化一个空对象emailMap 。 然后，它使用forEach()方法遍历用户数组，并将每个电子邮件到姓名的映射存储在emailMap对象中，其中电子邮件是键，姓名是值。

现在时间复杂度是O(n+m) 。 也就是说，上面的代码可能运行1000+100 = 1100

这种修改后的方法比原始版本更快，因为它减少了为每个电子邮件 ID 查找匹配用户对象所需的迭代次数。 该函数无需为每个电子邮件 ID 搜索用户数组，只需对用户数组执行一次迭代即可创建电子邮件到姓名的映射。 随后的查找是使用 emailMap 对象中的恒定时间键查找完成的。

感谢您的阅读，请在评论部分留下一个喜欢并分享您的想法。

快乐编码

一旦暂停，mrh0200 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，mrh0200 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，mrh0200 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 mrh0200 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 mrh0200 的方法：

降低 mrh0200 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 mrh0200 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

   

On working on a project i want to return array of names for the array of email ids i have.

const users = [ {name: 'John', email: 'john@example.com'}, {name: 'Jane', email: 'jane@example.com'}, {name: 'Bob', email: 'bob@example.com'}
]; function getNames(emailIds) { return emailIds.map((emailId)=>{ const user = users.find((user)=>{ return user.email === emailId }); return user?.name || emailId })
}

As you see the above code, the time complexity is O(n*m). In other words, assume the emailIds array is of size 100 and users array size is 1000 then the above code may run 1000*100 = 100000, so in order to reduce the iterations

function getNames(emailIds) { const emailMap= {}; users.forEach((user)=>{ emailMap[user.email]=user.name }); return emailIds.map((emailId)=>{ return emailMap[emailId] || emailId })
}

This function first initializes an empty object emailMap. It then iterates over the users array using the forEach() method and stores each email-to-name mapping in the emailMap object, where the email is the key and the name is the value.

Now the time complexity is O(n+m). In other words, the above code may run 1000+100 = 1100

This modified approach is faster than the original version because it reduces the number of iterations needed to find a matching user object for each email ID. Instead of searching the users array for each email ID, the function only needs to perform a single iteration over the users array to create the email-to-name mappings. Subsequent lookups are done using constant-time key lookups in the emailMap object.

Thank you for reading please leave a like and share your thoughts in the comment section.

Happy Coding

Francesco Ciulla - Feb 12

item.title: Elastic Search 和 Open Search——许可证大战简史 | Elastic Search and Open Search - a brief history of the license war
item.description:
云供应商与开源软件公司之间的开源许可之战

在CAP 定理中，ElasticSearch 属于CP 范畴，这意味着它优先考虑一致性和分区容错性而不是可用性。 这意味着在网络分区或故障的情况下，ElasticSearch 将选择保持数据的一致性而不是提供即时可用性，这可能会导致某些节点在分区解决之前变得不可用。

多年来，Elastic Search 经历了多次变化，包括其许可证的变化。 以下是这些变化的简要历史：

  • 许可证的这种变化引起了开源社区的担忧，一些人认为这违反了开源软件的原则。 作为回应，一组开发人员分叉了 Elastic Search 并创建了一个名为 Open Search 的新项目。 流行数据库的一些其他许可变更：

  • 该许可证增加了对未回馈开源项目的云提供商使用软件的限制。
  • 该许可证增加了对软件商业用途的限制。
  • 新许可证限制云提供商在不购买商业许可证的情况下将 CockroachDB 作为服务提供。
  • 新许可证增加了对云提供商如何使用该软件的限制，要求他们购买商业许可证才能将 Kafka 作为服务提供。

为什么 AWS 对此不满意？ 开放搜索为何诞生？

因为 Cloud Provider 将根据新的 lincese 收费。

作为对 Elastic NV 许可证变更的回应， AWS 宣布它将分叉 Elastic Search 并创建自己的软件版本，称为 OpenSearch。

开放搜索诞生

它包括一些 Elastic Search 中没有的新功能和改进，例如新的安全和治理功能。

除了 OpenSearch 之外，AWS 还提供其他几种开源数据库服务，包括 Amazon DocumentDB ，一个与 MongoDB 兼容的完全托管的文档数据库，以及 Amazon Neptune ，一个与 Neo4j 兼容的完全托管的图形数据库。

概括

虽然开源软件旨在免费提供和分发，但投资于其开发的公司可能会觉得他们没有获得公平的投资回报。 这导致引入了旨在解决此问题的新许可证。

围绕开源软件的使用和分发进行的持续辩论凸显了平衡开发人员、用户和投资开源软件的公司的需求所面临的挑战。 虽然开源软件实现了令人难以置信的创新和协作，但确保投资开源项目的公司能够维持其投资并获得投资回报也很重要。

随着开源软件的使用不断增长，我们很可能会继续看到围绕支持开源软件开发和分发的最佳方式的辩论和讨论。 尽管在此过程中可能存在挑战和分歧，但很明显，开源软件将继续在推动技术行业的创新和协作方面发挥关键作用。

参考：

一旦被暂停，aws-builders 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

取消暂停后，aws-builders 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 aws-builders：

降低 aws-builders 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

An Open Source License War Between Cloud Providers and Open Source Software Company

Elastic Search is a powerful search engine that was first released in 2010. It quickly gained popularity due to its ability to scale and handle large amounts of data. In 2015, Elastic Search was rebranded as the Elastic Stack, which includes additional tools such as Logstash, Beats, and Kibana.

In the CAP theorem, ElasticSearch falls into the CP category, which means that it prioritizes consistency and partition tolerance over availability. This means that in the event of a network partition or failure, ElasticSearch will choose to maintain consistency of data over providing immediate availability, which can cause some nodes to become unavailable until the partition is resolved.

What happened of Elastic Search’s License?

Over the years, Elastic Search has undergone several changes, including changes to its license. Here is a brief history of those changes:

  • 2014: Elastic Search was released under the Apache License, Version 2.0, which is a permissive open-source license.
  • 2018: Elastic NV, the company behind Elastic Search, introduced the Elastic License, which included additional restrictions on the use and distribution of the software.
  • In 2021, Elastic NV announced that it would be changing the license of Elastic Search and the Elastic Stack to a new license called the Server Side Public License (SSPL). The SSPL is similar to the license introduced by MongoDB in 2019, and it includes additional restrictions on the use of the software by cloud providers.

This change in license led to concerns within the open-source community, with some arguing that it goes against the principles of open-source software. In response, a group of developers forked Elastic Search and created a new project called Open Search. Open Search is a community-driven, open-source fork of Elastic Search that is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0.

Some Other License Changes of Popular Database:

  • In 2019, MongoDB, a popular document database, made a similar change to its license, introducing the Server Side Public License (SSPL). This license added restrictions on the use of the software for cloud providers that did not contribute back to the open source project.
  • In 2020, Neo4j, a graph database, also introduced a new license called the Commons Clause. This license added restrictions on the commercial use of the software.
  • In 2020, CockroachDB, a distributed SQL database, changed its license from the open-source Apache License 2.0 to a new license called the CockroachDB Community License. The new license restricts cloud providers from offering CockroachDB as a service without purchasing a commercial license.
  • In 2020, Confluent, the company behind the popular Apache Kafka messaging system, changed the license of its Confluent Platform from the open-source Apache License to the Confluent Community License. The new license adds restrictions on how the software can be used by cloud providers, requiring them to purchase a commercial license to offer Kafka as a service.

Why AWS is Not Happy With it? Why Open Search Was Born?

Because Cloud Provider will be charged under new lincese.

AWS played a key role in the license changes of Elastic Search, Open Search, MongoDB, and Neo4j. AWS is one of the largest users of Elastic Search and has contributed significantly to its development. In response to Elastic NV’s license change, AWS announced that it would be forking Elastic Search and creating its own version of the software, called OpenSearch.

Open Search was Born

OpenSearch is a community-driven, open-source project that is fully compatible with Elastic Search. It includes several new features and improvements that are not available in Elastic Search, such as new security and governance features.

In addition to OpenSearch, AWS also offers several other open-source database services, including Amazon DocumentDB, a fully managed document database that is compatible with MongoDB, and Amazon Neptune, a fully managed graph database that is compatible with Neo4j.

Summary

The fork of Elastic Search into Open Search highlights the ongoing debate around the use and distribution of open-source software. While open-source software is designed to be freely available and distributable, companies that invest in its development may feel that they are not getting a fair return on their investment. This has led to the introduction of new licenses that seek to address this issue.

Elastic Search and Open Search have undergone several changes over the years, including changes to their licenses. The recent change in license from Elastic NV has led to the creation of OpenSearch, a community-driven, open-source fork of Elastic Search. MongoDB and Neo4j have also introduced new licenses that restrict the use and distribution of their software. AWS played a key role in the forking of these open source databases, and continues to offer several open-source database services to its customers. The ongoing debate around the use and distribution of open-source software highlights the challenges of balancing the needs of developers, users, and companies that invest in open-source software. While open-source software has enabled incredible innovation and collaboration, it is also important to ensure that companies that invest in open-source projects are able to sustain their investments and receive a return on their investment.

As the use of open-source software continues to grow, it is likely that we will continue to see debates and discussions around the best ways to support the development and distribution of open-source software. While there may be challenges and disagreements along the way, it is clear that open-source software will continue to play a key role in driving innovation and collaboration in the technology industry.

References:

LABOUARDY Mohamed - Mar 3

Supratip Banerjee - Mar 3

Pubudu Jayawardana - Mar 2
